code-pyro-thermite.title: Thermite reagent (pyrotechnic.yml:17-37)
code-pyro-thermite.note: Thermite's only tileReactions entry is FlammableTileReaction (x2 temperature multiplier). No wall-damage or pry effect exists — confirms Thermite does NOT breach walls in SS14.

code-pyro-clf3-prytile.title: ChlorineTrifluoride PryTileReaction (pyrotechnic.yml:109)
code-pyro-clf3-prytile.note: ChlorineTrifluoride has PryTileReaction — the ONLY structural-damage tile reaction in vanilla SS14 chemistry. Backs the floor-pry strategy.

code-reagents-lead.title: Lead reagent in toxins.yml (no recipe, no plant, no dispenser)
code-reagents-lead.note: Searching the vanilla Reactions/ and Hydroponics/ folders for 'Lead' as a reaction product or seed chemical returns zero hits; Lead is defined as a reagent but not produced anywhere — confirming gobbygobbler's unobtainable claim for vanilla. UPDATE 2026-07-12 (D3 item-fill scan): ChemVendInventorySyndicate.contrabandInventory stocks ChemistryBottleLead x2 (30u) — the SyndieJuice chem vendor (AccessReader: SyndicateAgent) dispenses Lead bottles. No crew path still; classified antag-only, not unobtainable.

code-goob-licoxide-lead.title: Goob Station's Licoxide recipe (Lead added as reactant)
code-goob-licoxide-lead.note: Per config.py FORK_REGISTRY[goob].modified_reactions: 'Licoxide: Added Lead(1) as extra reactant'. Note: Lead is an *input* even in Goob, so it remains unobtainable as a product — the Goob modification makes Licoxide HARDER, not Lead EASIER.

code-chemicals-chloral.title: Chloral Hydrate recipe (chemicals.yml)
code-chemicals-chloral.note: Chloral: Chlorine + Ethanol + Water (all three from Chemical Dispenser). Backs silent-kill and sedation-ambush strategies.

code-reagents-potassium-water.title: Potassium-Water exothermic explosion reaction
code-reagents-potassium-water.note: K+H2O reaction in chemicals.yml produces an explosion via ChemExplosion with intensity 0.25 per unit, capped at 100. Backs mass-explosion strategy.

code-toxins-romerol.title: Romerol reagent (toxins.yml:458-475)
code-toxins-romerol.note: Romerol triggers CauseZombieInfection at min 5u, worksOnTheDead: true, contrabandSeverity: Syndicate. No recipe in any of the 12 reaction YAMLs — uplink-only. Backs zombie-outbreak strategy.

code-toxins-hemorrhinol.title: Hemorrhinol reagent (toxins.yml:766-783)
code-toxins-hemorrhinol.note: Hemorrhinol applies StatusEffectHemorrhage (7 bleed ticks, 21s) plus ModifyBleed amount 0.5. Backs bleed-drip strategy.

code-medicine-hemorrhinol-recipe.title: Hemorrhinol recipe (medicine.yml:677-687)
code-medicine-hemorrhinol-recipe.note: Heparin 2 + Razorium 2 + Plasma 1 → Hemorrhinol 2. Razorium itself is from CaninaseFelinaseReaction (fun.yml) — cross-dept via banana/lime juice.

code-narcotics-stimulants.title: Stimulants reagent (narcotics.yml:125-210)
code-narcotics-stimulants.note: Stimulants grant walk/sprint 1.25x, strip Stunned/Knockdown/Sleep, AdjustReagent ChloralHydrate -10 (purges sedatives). Backs stim-super-soldier self-buff strategy.

code-chemicals-stimulants-recipe.title: Stimulants recipe (chemicals.yml:318-330, minTemp 370)
code-chemicals-stimulants-recipe.note: Ephedrine 1 + Vestine 1 + Oxygen 2 @ minTemp 370 → Stimulants 2. Vestine is contraband (plant-mutation path), temp requires heater setup.

code-rucm-speed-demon.title: AU14DrugSpeedDemon reagent (RuCM drugs.yml:1-36)
code-rucm-speed-demon.note: Speed Demon: MovespeedModifier walk 1.3 / sprint 1.34, Narcotic metabolism 0.4, overdose 15, Poison 2/tick past 40u and 4/tick past 80u, AdjustReagent ChloralHydrate -10. Searching the fork's Reactions/ folders for AU14DrugSpeedDemon as a product returns zero hits — the drug has NO synthesis recipe.

code-rucm-speed-demon-sources.title: AU14DrugDealerDrugBottle in RuCM antag loadouts (antags.yml)
code-rucm-speed-demon-sources.note: The Speed Demon pill bottle (5x15u pills) ships in the antag drug dealer loadout — the primary in-game acquisition path.

code-rucm-weyu-crate.title: Speed Demon bottle in WeyU experiments crate (weyu_experiments.yml)
code-rucm-weyu-crate.note: Secondary acquisition path: the Weyland-Yutani experiments crate includes the Speed Demon pill bottle among its contents.

code-toxins-tazinide.title: Tazinide reagent (toxins.yml:668-683)
code-toxins-tazinide.note: Tazinide applies Electrocute effect at probability 0.5 per metabolism tick. Recipe in chemicals.yml: Licoxide + Vestine. Note: Licoxide's vanilla recipe is Lithium + Zinc (Goob adds Lead as extra reactant, making Licoxide harder there, not Lead easier).

code-chemicals-thermite-recipe.title: Thermite recipe (chemicals.yml:252-263, impact Medium)
code-chemicals-thermite-recipe.note: Iron 1 + Aluminium 1 + Oxygen 1 → Thermite 3. All dispenser-available. Backs thermite-breach strategy — despite SS13 folklore, Thermite in SS14 does NOT melt walls (see code-pyro-thermite), but its FlammableTileReaction x2 temperature makes it useful for burning through doors over time.

code-narcotics-teargas.title: TearGas reagent (narcotics.yml:400-458)
code-narcotics-teargas.note: TearGas inflicts TemporaryBlindness (min 4u), Cough, and MovementSpeedModifier 0.65 over area. Marked allowedDepartments: [Security] — vanilla access requires Sec role or loot.

code-narcotics-happiness.title: Happiness reagent (narcotics.yml:461-500)
code-narcotics-happiness.note: Happiness forces Laugh/Whistle/Crying emotes + Pax-style pacifism popups. Recipe in medicine.yml:624-637 — Laughter 2 + Epinephrine 1 + Ethanol 1 (+ Plasma 5 catalyst) → Happiness 4.

code-toxins-honk.title: Honk reagent (toxins.yml:573-602)
code-toxins-honk.note: Honk forces the Honk emote with force: true — target cannot suppress it. Combined with Happiness for coordinated clown-chaos radio/chat disruption.

code-fun-feline-canine.title: CaninaseFelinaseReaction (fun.yml)
code-fun-feline-canine.note: Felinase + Caninase → Razorium 2 + Radioactive explosion (maxIntensity 5). Dual-purpose: area rad damage AND produces Razorium as a follow-up weapon for Hemorrhinol. Recipe chain for each enzyme is deeply nested (Haloperidol + Psicodine + CarpoToxin/Happiness + Enzyme).

forum-gobby-killmix-2026.title: gobbygobbler: kill-mix feedback calling out Lead unobtainable in antag strategies
forum-gobby-killmix-2026.author: gobbygobbler
forum-gobby-killmix-2026.note: Forum thread where gobbygobbler reports that 'easy' difficulty strategies use reagents that are 50% unobtainium or botany-coop. Confirmed true for slow-poison (Lead) in vanilla.
forum-gobby-killmix-2026.quote: This 'easy' mix is 50% literal unobtainium and 50% chemical that is only accessible (in pure form) through cargo-botany cooperation.

forum-testicular-thermite-walls-2026.title: Testicular_Man: thermite breaching is SS13-only, not present on any SS14 fork
forum-testicular-thermite-walls-2026.author: Testicular_Man
forum-testicular-thermite-walls-2026.note: Forum post stating that thermite wall-breach mechanic does not exist in SS14 and never has. Corroborated by direct YAML inspection (see code-pyro-thermite).
forum-testicular-thermite-walls-2026.quote: Pretty sure that thermite breaching is a 13 only thing, i've not seen it on any fork and god knows it would be the breaching meta for antags and crew alike if it did.

forum-steelclaw-thermite-forks-2026.title: Steelclaw: asks which fork actually supports thermite wall-breach (expected: none)
forum-steelclaw-thermite-forks-2026.author: Steelclaw
forum-steelclaw-thermite-forks-2026.note: Forum post requesting per-fork clarification on thermite wall-breach — answer (confirmed by cross-fork YAML grep): no fork supports it. Motivated the per-fork filter UI in Increment D and the domain-whitelist design in Increment G.
forum-steelclaw-thermite-forks-2026.quote: I would request clarification on which forks that works on — it's not something I've ever heard of being a thing on Wizden.

mk-general-antag-playtime.title: Documented from maintainer's SS14 playtime
mk-general-antag-playtime.note: Original curator compiled these notes from personal playtime across multiple SS14 forks. Source is undocumented — community verification via PR is encouraged.

mk-shift-presets.title: Shift-start preset curation from maintainer's playtime
mk-shift-presets.note: Reagent picks and amounts for the one-click shift-start presets (Batch Planner). Compiled from personal playtime; every reagent id is validated against extracted vanilla recipes at build time. Community tuning via PR is encouraged.

mk-species-guide.title: Species physiology notes from maintainer's playtime
mk-species-guide.note: Breathing gases and physiology quirks per playable species. Species/body prototypes are not part of the extraction manifest (yet) — unlike per-reagent organ conditions, which ARE extracted from reagent YAML. Corrections via PR encouraged.

mk-botany-guide.title: Cross-pollination / swab guide from maintainer's playtime
mk-botany-guide.note: The swab and mutation mechanics live in C# systems (MutationSystem, cross-pollination), not in YAML — unlike the mutation TARGET list, which is extracted from seed prototypes. The guide text is playtime knowledge; corrections via PR are encouraged.

antagStrategies.name: Flash & Grab
antagStrategies.desc: Flash Powder (Al+K+S) blinds everyone in an area. Use for smash-and-grab operations or escape cover.

antagStrategies.name: Floor Breach (CLF3)
antagStrategies.desc: Myth-buster: SS14 chemistry has NO wall-melting mechanic — the classic Thermite wall-breach is SS13 folklore. The only structural-damage tile reaction in SS14 chemistry is CLF3's PryTileReaction (pyrotechnic.yml:109). Spilling Chlorine Trifluoride pries floor tiles open — useful for breaching into subfloor pipes or lower decks. Extremely dangerous to the user.
antagStrategies.method: Splash or foam-deploy on target floor tile
loreWarnings: Method 'Splash or foam-deploy on target floor tile' does not match any known DELIVERY_MECHANISMS key

antagStrategies.name: CLF3 Armageddon
antagStrategies.desc: Chlorine Trifluoride — the ultimate SS14 destruction chemical. Unique PryTileReaction pries floor tiles when spilled (pyrotechnic.yml:109). Bloodstream deals Heat(2)+Poison(1)+Caustic(0.5); Flammable on touch with scream/popup reactions. Does NOT melt walls. Synthesize above 370K in a shatterproof container or it WILL explode in your hands.

antagStrategies.name: Tazinide Tase
antagStrategies.desc: Tazinide electrocutes the target every metabolism tick (50% probability) — effectively chain-stuns through armor that would block a stun baton. Recipe is Licoxide + Vestine. antagStrategies.name: Stim Super-Soldier
antagStrategies.desc: Self-buff stack for the antag. Stimulants grant 1.25x speed, purge ChloralHydrate/Knockdown/Sleep, and adrenaline-ignores slow-on-damage; Dexalin prevents asphyxiation during the sprint; Tricordrazine heals Brute+Burn. Turns a fragile traitor into a 30-second rampage threat. Requires Stimulants synthesis at 370K (heater setup).

antagStrategies.name: Thermite Door Breach
antagStrategies.desc: Contrary to SS13 folklore, Thermite in SS14 does NOT melt walls — but its FlammableTileReaction with x2 temperature multiplier can burn through airlock doors over time. Recipe is pure dispenser: Iron 1 + Aluminium 1 + Oxygen 1 → Thermite 3. Safer infiltration alternative to ChlorineTrifluoride (see floor-pry / clf3-armageddon).
antagStrategies.method: Spray bottle or splash on target door
loreWarnings: Method 'Spray bottle or splash on target door' does not match any known DELIVERY_MECHANISMS key
Desc mentions 'melting walls' — SS14 chemistry has no wall-melting mechanic

antagStrategies.name: Speed Demon Pills (RuCM)
antagStrategies.desc: Russian Marine Corps exclusive self-buff. The Speed Demon street drug gives +30% walk / +34% sprint speed while it metabolizes (0.4u/tick, ~37s per 15u pill) and strips Chloral Hydrate 10u/tick — a walking sedation counter. It CANNOT be synthesized: no reaction exists in the fork's chemistry. Known sources are the antag drug dealer's labeled bottle (5x15u pills) and the Weyland-Yutani experiments crate. Overdose at 15u and poison damage past 40u in the bloodstream — swallow one pill at a time, never stack.

Syringe.desc: Direct injection — target sees attacker and gets a combat log message. 15u capacity.

Hypospray.desc: Silent injection, no combat message to bystanders. 30u capacity. Requires CMO/medical access.

SmokeBomb.desc: Phosphorus+Potassium+Sugar reaction disperses ALL beaker chemicals as smoke cloud covering a wide area.

FoamGrenade.desc: Fluorosurfactant+Water creates foam carrying all other chemicals. Spreads on floor, makes surfaces slippery.

Food.desc: Inject reagents into food items. Target consumes voluntarily — zero suspicion if undetected.

Drink.desc: Slip reagents into existing drinks. Flavor may give it away — use tasteless chemicals for best results.

Pill.desc: Pill capsule holding up to 50u. Can be color-matched to look like medicine. Victim must swallow.

Beaker.desc: Standard beaker (50u). Throw at target to splash contents. Visible and obvious attack.

LargeBeaker.desc: Large beaker (100u). More capacity for bigger splash area.

BluespaceBeaker.desc: Bluespace beaker (300u). Enormous capacity — enough chemicals for a devastating attack.

SyndicateHypospray.desc: Looks like a normal hypospray but is a Syndicate tool. Silent injection, 25u capacity. Available from traitor uplink.

Telecrystal.desc: Currency for the traitor uplink. Spend on tools, weapons, and equipment. Not a chemical delivery device.

shiftPresets.name: Med-Chem Starter
shiftPresets.desc: Classic shift-opening batch: brute/burn/tox coverage plus a crit stabilizer.

shiftPresets.name: Advanced Meds
shiftPresets.desc: Second wave once basics are stocked: stronger burn care, airloss, radiation, cellular damage and body-temp stabilization.

shiftPresets.name: Botany Kit
shiftPresets.desc: Grow-room chemistry: nutrients, yield booster, controlled mutations and weed control.

shiftPresets.name: Bar Prep
shiftPresets.desc: Mixable bases to open the bar: soft-drink stock, ice and house vodka.

botanyGuide.title: Swabs & mutations — how the evolution chart works

sections.h: Species mutations (the arrows in the chart)
sections.body: Raise a plant's mutation level — most reliably by dosing the tray with Unstable Mutagen (~1-5u; more risks killing the plant) — and on the next growth ticks the plant may jump to one of its listed mutation targets. Only species with arrows here can jump; the targets come straight from the game's seed data.

Use a gauze swab on flowering plant A to collect its pollen, then swab plant B: B's next generation mixes STATS (potency, yield, lifespan, harvest type, chemicals) between the two. Swabs shuffle traits — they do NOT trigger the species jumps shown in the chart.

1) Mutagen small doses until the species you want appears. 2) Stabilize: harvest, replant the mutated seeds. 3) Swab-breed your best specimens to stack potency/yield onto the new species. 4) Robust Harvest boosts potency; seedless traits need the clippers workaround.

Human.name: Human
Human.note: Baseline physiology — all standard medicine applies.

Slime.name: Slime person
Slime.note: Slime metabolism — several reagents behave differently (see per-reagent organ notes).

Vox.name: Vox
Vox.note: Breathes NITROGEN; oxygen is toxic to them — avoid oxygen-based breathing meds; internals with N2 required off-station.

Diona.name: Diona
Diona.note: Plant-based multi-organ physiology; regenerates in light, several meds metabolize oddly.

Moth.name: Moth person
Moth.note: Insectoid — pesticides (e.g. Bug Spray) poison them; can eat cloth.
